preamble: The transformation from a uvular nasal to an alveolar flap is a documented process of fronting and manner change, most notably observed as allophonic variation in Japanese where the moraic nasal interacts with following liquids.

examples.note: In rapid speech, the uvular nasal often assimilates or shifts toward the position of a following alveolar flap liquid.

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
Labrune, L. (2012). The Phonology of Japanese.
